item.title: Khosru returns home after hospital stay
item.description: After four days of treatment, Finance Minister Amir Khosru Mahmud Chowdhury has returned home from Gulshan’s United Hospital. On Wednesday, his personal assistant Shahiduzzaman said doctors advised the minister to rest, though he continues urgent official work from home. “He managed c

item.title: Iran could disrupt the Strait of Hormuz with drones for months
item.description: Iranian drone attacks could disrupt the Strait of Hormuz for months, but how long the Islamic Republic could sustain its missile barrage is less clear, according to intelligence sources and military analysts. Since the US and Israel attacked Iran on Saturday, Iran has launched hundreds of missiles

item.title: Apple debuts $599 MacBook Neo to challenge Chromebooks, Windows PCs
item.description: Apple on Wednesday unveiled the MacBook Neo, a lower-priced addition to its laptop lineup starting at $599, as it looks to broaden its reach in a price-sensitive PC market while rivals face tighter supply of memory chips. A lower-priced laptop marks one of Apple’s most aggressive entry point

item.title: New SPs appointed for six districts, 10 police officers transferred
item.description: The government has overhauled the police administration in six districts, including Bogura and Sylhet, as part of a wider reassignment of 10 officers holding the rank of superintendent of police (SP). Among those moved, four serving SPs have been withdrawn from their district postings and reassign

item.title: Israeli airlines to increase flights to Tel Aviv this week
item.description: Two of Israel's airlines said they would start to resume flights to Ben Gurion International Airport near Tel Aviv as the country's airspace is expected to gradually reopen later on Wednesday. Israir, one of Israel's two smaller carriers along with Arkia, said it planned five flights to Ben Gurion

item.title: Islamic University teacher murder: Revenge over transfer alleged as motive
item.description: An employee of Islamic University (IU) has allegedly stabbed the chairperson of the social welfare department to death in her office before attempting to take his own life, in an incident authorities believe was triggered by a grievance over a departmental transfer. The victim, Assistant Professor

item.title: Asif Mahmud reveals Tk 1 million bank balance
item.description: Former advisor Asif Mahmud Shojib Bhuyain has revealed that he holds just under Tk 1 million in his bank accounts after authorities sought details of his financial records. The National Citizen Party (NCP) spokesperson disclosed his own and his family members’ bank accounts at a press confer

item.title: Combined auditing system soon to relieve taxpayers' pain, plug tax evasion
item.description: Taxpayers may soon sigh with relief from the rigours of responding separately to multiple queries from tax and VAT officials as the government's revenue authority is integrating its outmoded auditing system. A joint audit system for income tax and VAT (value-added tax) payers is set to

item.title: Former DGFI chief, two other Army officers appointed as ambassadors
item.description: The government has transferred three serving major generals from the Bangladesh Army to the foreign ministry to assign them as ambassadors. The officers are Maj Gen Nasim Parvez, Maj Gen Mohammad Kamrul Hasan, and Maj Gen Mohammad Jahangir Alam. The Ministry of Public Administration issued a noti

item.title: BGB on high alert at borders over fuel smuggling risk
item.description: Border Guard Bangladesh (BGB) has been put on maximum alert along the borders to prevent fuel smuggling amid volatility in the international market triggered by the ongoing Iraq-Iran war. According to BGB headquarters sources, uncertainty in global fuel supply centering the Iraq-Iran conflict has

item.title: Stability, welfare, and confidence
item.description: As a new government assumes office, Bangladesh begins another important chapter in its development journey. Elections renew political leadership; they also renew public expectations. Citizens will judge this new term not by the scale of promises made, but by the stability maintained, the welfare de

item.title: Breaking the public financing constraint
item.description: Bangladesh's rapid infrastructure expansion has strained fiscal and financial capacity. Public debt reached US$ 174 billion as of June 2025, driven largely by infrastructure financed through budget allocations, bank borrowing, and concessional external loans. At the same time, the banking sector re
